Okay, what we're going to do now is go into a different chunk. When it comes to content and when it comes to conducting your meeting, let me just give you something about conducting your meeting. I like to tell everyone in my meetings that we stick to a very strict schedule. Most people pay me to come to my trainings and I tell them up front. I give a 15 minute break and at 15 minutes and one second I start. If you want to get your money's worth, you be in your seat. I am not a person who goes out and solicits you to come back into our room. If you can understand the importance of sticking to a schedule when you become a speaker in front of your own room, you're going to see how terrible it is to try to get people back in the room. So I'll give you breaks but make sure you come back in. We've also changed the temperature to make sure that you're a little bit more comfortable in here. I know some of you came in late and if you need to see Janet and if you have not registered or taken care of that matter, if you would see Michael in the back of the room, he'll make sure that you can get everything in order of that matter. Take care. Okay, in learning more of the chunks, I'm going to kind of look at a couple of my notes. I don't speak from notes and we might address that a little bit occasionally but I have certain chunks that I want to make sure that I cover. I know the chunks inside and out, I'm just going to make sure that I cover them. How many of you know how to surf? Not the internet. How many of you know how to surf on water? Well how many of you have ever seen someone surf? Okay, now we're on the railway. Okay now, if you went to the beach and you're seeing all these people surf out there and it enters your mind, you know, I'd like to surf. I'd like to have that thrill of just sailing down on that wave on that board, having everybody look at me and then just gradually going up on that sand. That's got to be a marvelous feeling. Now is there something between I think I'd like to do that and doing that? Is it called lack of knowledge? Okay now, let's think about it. The reason I picked the surf in the example is because very few people know how to surf. So let's say, how would we learn how to surf? So I want to kind of put you in a picture here and show you where we are. You're out in the water. You're sitting on your board. Bobbing. Waiting. What are you waiting on? A wave. Now are you waiting on just any wave or? The big one. Or the big one. The tsunami, right? Okay now, so now you're waiting on this wave. How many of you feel like you've just been in that this is almost like real life, that you've just been kind of bobbing, waiting in life? And what are we waiting on in life? Oh, the big opportunity that's going to come along, right? Now if you would like to learn to surf, now we're all out here in the same position. You're on the board already. You're sitting there, you're bobbing and you're waiting. What would be the first step in the process of surfing? Pardon? Take a chance. I don't know how my body does that, but that's philosophical and I appreciate that. But what's the process in actually getting up on that board and surfing? Keep the eyes open. Okay, I'm looking and here it comes. It's getting bigger and bigger and closer and closer. Now it's time to make a decision. What are you going to do? Get up. Get up. I thought you had just a paddle and a board. You got a paddle. You got a teacher. Got a teacher. I can tell I have got a philosophical audience here. You got all these really warm and fuzzy thoughts process. You're on your board and you got to do something. What are you going to do? I would propose to you that the first thing you need to do is point your board toward the beach. Now we laugh, but is that extremely important? Is it critical in your life to know where you are going? Time out. What did we just do in the first part of our presentation? Let's go back. We showed the light at the end of the tunnel. We brought out of our audience where they wanted to go, right? And we painted, let them paint a picture where they wanted to go. Did we not? Let's learn the process of doing something. Let's learn the process of surfing. What's the first step in the process of surfing? I could have said, what's the first step in the process of doing anything? I didn't say that. I said, what's the first step in the process of surfing? If you wanted to surf successfully, what's the first thing you should do? What's the answer? Point your board toward the beach. You going to start paddling? Oh, you need to get up off your rear end and get in position to make things happen. Time out. One of the things that you can do in your seminars if you would like to have a powerful seminar. Now, what I just modeled for you was a couple of things. Was a struggle to get you back in the room. Did you feel an awkwardness in my talking about that? Did you feel a little chastisement on my part for not being back in your room? Okay, I'm trying to role model everything for you. You will struggle to get your audience back in the room. I have a suggestion for you. There are two suggestions. Number one, in the beginning of a training you can tell everyone we stick to our schedule and I'm raising my hand in the air. Would everyone please raise your hand in the air? Now when you see all the hands in the air, stop what you're talking about and sit down and you'll be amazed at how effective that becomes. Okay? That gets everyone quiet. It doesn't get back in the room because they don't see the hands. Here is something that works extremely effective but it's going to cost you about $40. What we would do is we would take $1 bills and we would tape them underneath seats throughout the audience. And we would take two fives and place them one on this side, one on this side. It would take $1, $10 bill and it always went in this chair right here. Second chair inside on the front row to my right. Okay? So I would have 10, two fives, it's 20 and I had $21 bills. Okay? I am the star. Now if you understand what I'm saying, I, we're talking about you. You're the star. Okay? I don't want to struggle to get my audience back in. That is not what the star should do. So what you do is you have someone who is introduced. If Michael had introduced me, then I would have Michael come back and his job is to struggle to get everybody back in here. And it is a struggle, isn't it? And he would do this. And he'd say, let's all get back and let's go to it. But I am telling you, it is a struggle. You need to plan for this struggle and make sure that you handle it in a way to get things because when you come back home, you're the star. When you come back home, you want everybody to be in their seats and ready to go. Would you agree with me on that? Okay? So this is what Michael would do or whoever is introducing you. This is the role that they would do. Are we having a good time? Are we learning things? You know, one of the things that I have learned from working with John Childress so much is that you never know where your next money-making opportunity might come from. Now if I told you that you could be sitting on a money-making opportunity right now, what's the first thing you would do? And let me tell you something, your audience will not move. You're moving because I told you about having money's in the future. Isn't it amazing the power of knowledge? And Michael would experience a situation where people don't get it. You get off your rear end, you get in, get on your knees, on the board. You get in position to do what? Now what are you going to start doing? Paddling. Now, why are you paddling? To get into motion? What's another word for that? Building momentum. Now, tell me, why do you need momentum to accomplish anything? Why do you need momentum? Because see, when you are headed in the same direction as the force, okay. Here comes the wave. Let me tell you something. Let's get our timing down. Here comes the wave. It's getting closer and closer and closer, bigger and bigger. This is it. When do you start building your momentum before the wave gets here, as the wave is here, or after it is gone by? When? Before. Before. Put a big star by when do you build your momentum? This is critical to every presentation, okay. Time back in. You build your momentum. So now that you are building your momentum, the wave is coming, and as you build your momentum, then when the wave gets here, you're able to take advantage of this wave. And it just starts giving you the energy and you are surfing and you are just having the best time. You're reaching all your heart's desire ever had. You're now being fulfilled. Now, if you wanted to surf, what did we learn from this? That there is a process that you must learn. Do we have any engineers in the room? Okay, what kind of engineer are you? Electrical engineer. Second, I said, I don't want to know the process of becoming an engineer. I want to know the process of becoming a multimillionaire. And you can't learn just anything you want to learn. There are certain things that you need to study if you want to be financially independent. And you can't just walk through life and say, okay, by the way, I'm a jack of all trades. That's good. Oh, that's good stuff. I'm glad I learned that. That's good information. And then one day, boom, you're an engineer? No. Well, it shouldn't surprise you why most people never become a millionaire. Because you don't become a millionaire by just walking through life with good information and then all day you want to say, boom, I'm going to be a millionaire. First, you need to decide to become a millionaire. And you need to point your board in that direction. And you need to learn the process. You need to learn the strategies that will take you to financial independence. And by the way, as you are learning, you can start earning. How many of you have seen more than two opportunities? Ah, they keep coming, don't they? Okay, I want everyone in here to listen and learn from this. You see, when you have your momentum, any wave is an opportunity for you. Have you ever heard that the rich get richer and the poor get poorer? The poor people have no momentum. Opportunities keep coming. They're always here. They keep coming. But if you have no momentum, you just bob and bitch. Bob and bitch. Look at that guy. He's surfing right by me. It's a pressure cooker. And at the top of this pressure cooker is a release valve. All the pressure comes out the top. Wouldn't that be great? The answer at the top of the thing is to buy your product. You are trying to create a presentation that builds a fire under these people. You've got to do something with it. Then you need to put the fear of God in them about doing nothing. How do you do that? Wham, wham, wham. The Jaws music. You know, the greatest fear, I mean, the greatest risk in the world is to do nothing. And then you can give the definition of insanity. Einstein said, insanity is defined as continuing to do what you've always done, but you expect to get different results. Now, in this theme here, I'm going to give you several phrases that you can say the same thing. If you continue to go, if you continue to do what you're doing right now, where will you be financially in five years? Same place. If you continue to do what you're doing right now financially, where will you be financially in five years? Same place. Make them say it, make them feel it. Now, are you paying a picture now that if you do not do something, you cannot see the lighted end of the tunnel. Is this a very interesting presentation you are giving? And people will sit there and say this is awesome stuff. They'll be writing notes like crazy, and you're going to feel so good, I'm giving them good information. Now, you know, I can give you good information and it does not build a fire under you. I'm choosing the information twofold. I want you to learn I want to build a fire. Now, guess what? It's going to kill my cells. What are some of the pressure leaks? Oh, I want to think about it. Oh, my gosh. If I say, oh, that's okay, you can go home and think about it. Psssss I got to check with my accountant. Psssss You hear about two guys in the balloon? They just kind of floating in this hot air balloon up through there. And all of a sudden this wind comes, kind of like last week. Wind comes along and it takes that hot air balloon all over the place and they have no idea where they are. They just sold that high. And then pretty soon the wind calms down, it starts floating back down and they come back down to Earth but they have no clue, you know, what location they're in. So they see this guy down there and they barely see it's a man down there and he says, Hey, where are we? You're in a balloon. The guy looks at it and he says, That's a CPA. He said, How can you tell he's a CPA? Barely see him. He says, Cause his answer was absolutely correct but totally worthless. Psssss Should you know what pressure leaks are out there? Shouldn't in your presentation you start saying things that would plug those holes even before they ever come up? Would that be smart? Okay, and let me... By the way, can I ask you a question, Susan? Okay, I notice you're wearing a blue blazer. If I asked you what color your blue blazer is right now, what would the answer be? What would the correct answer be? Blue blazer is blue. It's blue. Now, two weeks from today, if you were wearing that and I said, What color is your blazer? What would the correct answer be? It's blue. So some things, no matter how long you think about them, the answer is always the same, isn't it? It's never a mistake to learn to make more money. Thank you very much. Is that a chunk you can use for the rest of your life? What plug did you... What hole did that just plug? I want to think about it. I want to think about it. Okay, so we're building a pressure cooker here. We're trying to get them to take action. Now I want to give you what I consider the most powerful chunk that you will use in your presentation. And as I give it to you, you can probably tell which of your favorite speakers I have worked with. And you'll see variations of this in all good presentations. It's called the Think and Grow Rich Chunk. How many of you have read the book Think and Grow Rich, by the way? Is that a great book? What is it? It's just the standard. It's like the Bible for success. It's been around what, written in the what, the 30s or something like that? Now, for those of you who haven't read the book, there's a man named Napoleon Hill, that the wealthiest man in the world, Andrew Carnegie at that time, wanted him to study wealthy people with one thought in mind. Do wealthy people have similar characteristics? What makes them wealthy was the question. Napoleon Hill spent years and years studying this. And when he finished the study, he came to two unique discoveries. And number one, he found out that yes, they do have certain things in common. And he wrote a book called, he says they have 16 characteristics in common. Isn't that interesting? You want to be wealthy? Then you better be born with those 16 characteristics. But his second discovery was more phenomenal. No one was born with those elements of success. So what was so astounding that he wrote in the book, Think and Grow Rich, was twofold. All successful people have 16 characteristics in common. And none of them were born with those characteristics. Every successful person developed those characteristics. Time out. Is that powerful so far? Okay, number one, I am not teaching. Who's teaching now? Napoleon Hill. Is he the authority? Yes. Okay, so whatever I talk about now, this is the authority, and nobody's pointing to me. You're letting someone else do your teaching now. Five of those 16 characteristics are so important that I would like to cover them today. Number one, you must have a burning desire. A burning desire. Now we talked about financial independence, and we talked about having the things money could provide us. Remember when we did that just a few moments ago? Now, do you really want that? Or are you just saying, yeah, I'll take that. Is that a burning desire? I'll take that. Absolutely not. Because do you think that there might be obstacles between where you are now and where you'd like to be financially? Absolutely. Now what do you think carries some people over obstacles, through them, around them, or whatever? What is it? It's that burning desire of an athlete that says, I will be the best, and I practice, and I practice, and I work out, and I work out. And that burning desire is what carries them through the obstacles other people kind of give up. Do you really want to be wealthy? Do you really? When we talk about what you do, would you really help other people? Do you think you'd be a better human being, a stronger human being if you helped others? Do you really want that position of financial independence? That's called the burning desire. Now, is that just flip a switch, say, yep, okay, I got that, got that down. You know, I don't know where you grew up, but I grew up in what's called the Bible Belt in the South. Wealthy people were few, and as you grew up and you go to church, you were taught. From the time I learned, when I went to church, that money was a root of all evil. The Bible said, you ever know what Bible thumpers are? The Bible says, and they thump you on the head with what the Bible says. The Bible says money is a root of all evil. Is that what the Bible said? No, no, no, no, no. You mean people misquote things? Yes. Absolutely. Now, if you didn't have any money and you wanted to teach kids why you didn't have any money, what was the Bible said? We don't want to be up there where I can't go. The Bible says that the money is a root of, that the love of money is a root of all evil. Now, let's talk about the Bible just for a moment. If you took the Bible and you made it into a movie script, it's a pretty interesting movie, right? Does it have any main characters in this movie? I mean, when you look at the main characters, and you've got Moses and Jacob and Job and Solomon, what all these main characters have in common? Rich can't even describe these people, right? The Bible describes how much money they had. They owned all as far as the eye could see. Is that a lot? That's more than my 500 acres in Mississippi. I can tell you that right now. As far as the eye could see, they had so much money that they had to leave the silver on the outside of city gates because there wasn't any more room on the inside. That's where they had to go, right? Now, these people have a lot of money. Solomon, was Solomon a wise man? Was he a wealthy man? This dude had and supported over a thousand wives. Is that a lot of money? You can't look for that one, too. Okay, you see, when I read the Bible, I see a different picture now. I see that the main characters had a lot of money and what happens is when you know the characteristics of successful people, you're not successful because you have a lot of money. You have a lot of money because you're successful. Money just comes to people who have those 16 characteristics. One of them happens to be a burning desire. The second element of success that I want to talk to you about is called specialized knowledge. Napoleon Hill found out that people that made a lot of money and became very successful took one area and said, why don't I just concentrate on one area and get really good at that and become specialized at that? Now, Bill Gates, is he a wealthy man? Now, he's specialized in software. For IBM, the big things are just a plane. I'm going to specialize in software for a personal computer. Is that like highly specialized and all of a sudden, the wealthiest person in the world? Is that specialized knowledge? Okay, could you become specialized? I'm just going to invest in real estate. I'm just going to specialize in building a down line. I'm just going to specialize in going out and setting up elderly health care centers. Whatever it is, it's called specialized knowledge. The third element of success is discipline. By the way, when you go home and you tell people, I went to a seminar about becoming wealthy, are they going to be really supportive of you? Oh, what a wonderful thing. I wish I could have gone. That's so smart of you. You should be commended because you want to improve yourself. Is that what they're saying? Oh, yeah, right. You went to one of those things, right? Mm-hmm. Now, if the people around you are trying to tear you down, do you think discipline might be a unique characteristic and important in your career? If you told someone that, you know, there's a process to becoming wealthy, and I'm going to learn that process. And if people start saying, no, you shouldn't do that, are you going to have to have discipline to stick to your decision? How many of you have already made a decision that you're going to learn how to become wealthy? Could I see a show of hands on that? Time out. If they make their mind up now, when they get down here to the opportunity, when the wave is coming, and they know they need to build a momentum, when this gets down here and the pressure is so much pressure here, they can't stand it anymore, you do not want them to have lack of discipline. You want them to stick to their guns. Then you need to teach people about discipline early in their life. You know, in the Bible, it talks about raise a child in the way that they should go, and when they're old, they will not depart from it. That's exactly what you do in a presentation. You teach an audience what they need to know, and when they get out of here, when they get down a position, they will not depart from it. That is a principle that you need to understand, that you've got to teach people what they need to do. Now, I'm going to interrupt myself, and I'm going to ask you something right from the heart. Are you seeing right now the power that you could abuse? Absolutely. And I'm telling you, with this information, you can sell anything to anybody, but should you? So you need to be careful of what it is you're going to offer them. But what would it do to your heart? What would it do to the way you present this information? If you knew that if they got this information, that it literally would change their life positively, that it would bring their families closer together. Now, that's what you're looking for. Don't sell stuff. Be a prophet. I have information for you. This information will change your life, but make sure it is information that will change their life. Now, time back again. Don't abuse what we're going to be talking about. The third element is discipline. The fourth element that is so important I want to cover today is you must become decisive in nature. Wealthy people have developed the characteristic of gathering information, analyzing information, and making the decision right there, and sticking with that decision. Now, time out. Watch my body language. What do you want people to do right here in your presentation? Make a decision and not deviate from it, right? They need to know where they want to go, have a burning desire to get there, stick to their decision, but what is it they're getting here? Specialized knowledge. They need to become decisive in nature. You remember when I started earlier, I said, you start here. What do you want to have happen here? They got to make a decision. Their mindset needs to have no, all plugs have been plugged. There is no release valves here. The only thing that has not been addressed, the only option they have is to get this system, this method, this process, this way, whatever word you feel comfortable with. That's the only acceptable answer. That's the only thing their mind can understand. This is my opportunity in life. If that's the position you want these people to be in right now, then you better start plugging these things in your timeline. Now, sir, when you said, I'm running out of time and I'm plugging these things in, do you understand why I said you shouldn't have been plugging anything in yet, right? So that's what I'm saying. I'm right down here. If this is what you want, then what parts of this presentation plug that in? It makes no difference what order you put it in. Any of these chunks will build this. The fourth element of success is you must become decisive in nature. The fifth element of success is that all successful people surround themselves with a mastermind team. Time out! What would be a great thing to offer someone if you really want to change your life? How about, by the way, is it unknown how do I build a mastermind team? How do I do it? Who do I need on there? How many of you would like to have access to my mastermind team? I mean, I'm a multi-millionaire and I've already got my team built. Wouldn't it make sense that instead of you building yours, you just kind of absorb, it'd come right into the fold and you've got to have access to all of that? You know, I had many, many wealthy people in this country that as they were building their mastermind team, they did not go and learn anything about commercial real estate investing. They did not. You know why? Because I was on a team. Why do they need to go out and learn what I already know if I am on the team? Is a mastermind team powerful? Is there a big difference between what you know and what we know? Huge! Then you need to become part of a team. Time out. If you were selling an enlightened millionaire program, if you were selling a mentorship program, if you were selling any kind of product that allowed people to become part of a group, is that important to their success? But what if you invited them into an awesome group of people? People who cared, people who would share, would they do better when anybody there would help them go through their problem? Do you feel good about what you are selling, is my point? There are five elements of success there. Burning desire, specialized knowledge, discipline. You must become decisive in nature. And you have to surround yourself with a mastermind team. Now, as you deliver that chunk, you won't get to say all the things that I said, time out. And I would talk to you. You are delivering the five things. This is good information that they did. See, here is the thing. They don't know just yet where they are going to get that specialized knowledge. Will they know soon? Yes. They don't know just yet that they are going to have a problem discipline themselves. They don't know just yet that they have to become decisive. Have you ever had somebody say, like we talked to her about it, I want to think about it? Now, if you taught this powerful chunk in your presentation about what Napoleon Hill said about being decisive, now, how many of you were taught to sleep on it before you made a major decision? And who taught you that? And how much money did that? You know, my dad told me, son, you don't ever make a major financial decision without sleeping on it. Now, how many opportunities he missed while he was thinking about it? Some things, the answer is always the same. Some things the answer is always the same, no matter how long you sleep on it. But if you are not decisive, that opportunity passes you by. And I know that each of you has seen it time and time again. So when there is an opportunity, what should you do? Seize that opportunity. And will people be supportive in that decision? No, so you need to stick to your discipline. And when you see an opportunity, you go for it. You be decisive. And you head toward that worthwhile goal that you have set for you and your family. And you surround yourself with like-minded people. I think the biggest change in my life happened when I quit hanging around loser mentality and I started hanging around winning mentality. And you can tell the difference when a subject comes up if everybody starts telling you everything in the world is wrong with it versus everything in the world is good about it. When you start hanging around, my dad had a rule, son, you will not hang around with a pack of wolves. His definition of a pack of wolves was male people, boys. He said, you hang around a bunch of boys, you will get into trouble. Every time I got in trouble, I was with a bunch of boys. He said, I will allow you to use the family car. I will give you money to go on a date. I want you to date as many girls as you can. I never got in trouble when I was with a girl. Did I hear a couple of them getting in trouble? Is that what I heard? Brenda won't even let me date. Why do you think a parent might have a rule like that? By the way, was that a good rule? Absolutely. What I said, I didn't say my father was not a good advisor. I said he wasn't a good financial advisor. The things your parent teaches you, you can't even put a price on. But somewhere, sometime you have to understand are there limits to what you should listen to from your parents? You know, your career is going to be one of the first ones. Because most people, as they grow up, they desire a higher position than their parents had. And their parents don't understand that. And you have to break from that and get out on your own. And it was very tough for me to just say, I need to forget everything my dad taught me about finances. And I need to go out on my own and make my own decisions. Now the chunk called Think and Grow Rich. I was working with Robert Allen as his business partner when we launched the book Multiple Streams of Income. And we no longer business partners, but that's a totally different story. And I remember Robert Allen's words to me, he says, John, if this doesn't work out as a business, I want us to leave as friends. And I really appreciate it because as far as I'm concerned, he's just one of my best friends, right today. He has been so inspirational to me. A lot of you don't know Robert Allen, but if you do, he's written a book, Nothing Down, Multiple Streams of Income, Creating Wealth. The One Minute Millionaire is an awesome individual. And as I was working with him on developing his presentation, we did 16 cities in a row. You're talking about a grueling book tour. I was teaching with Think and Grow Rich Chunk. And I thought it was a powerful chunk, the way I taught it to him. The way he interpreted it and delivers it just blows me away. And instead of saying, like Napoleon Hill says, you need a burning desire, Robert Allen says, you need a vivid vision. And his ability to walk an audience through seeing where they want to go is second to none. I'll quote from what I've heard him say. Where would you like to be? I want you to look and see yourself in five years. Five years from today, what kind of house do you own? You just came home from going out to dinner and you're driving up in your driveway. What does the front of that house look like? You're parking your car right in front of the front door. What do those doors look like? Are they big, tall? Do they have leaded glass? Are they double doors? Are they heavy, lightweight? What do those doors look like? Now that door opens smooth. And when it opens and you walk into the foyer of your home five years from now, what does it smell like? What do you hear? Do you hear music? What do you see? You see a stairwell? Is it a winding stairwell? Now is the kitchen to your right or to your left? Now time out. Has he walked you into specifics? Are you seeing exactly to your own right? Are you seeing the house and the home of your dreams? He's a master at that. Can you duplicate that same thing? Can you ask the same questions? Okay, so as you're talking about, as you learn a chunk, you adapt it to however you feel. By the way, I have seen my students drop the name Think and Grow Rich. And they take credit themselves. I have no problem with that. Because if you'd like to be truly successful, you need to understand that there are five laws of success that you cannot violate. Number one. Is that another introduction? Can you teach the same five points? I prefer to let Napoleon Hill do my teaching. Because say I don't need the ego trip. I'm not saying that everybody needs an ego trip, but I don't need the ego trip. I let Napoleon Hill do my teaching because I never said you need to become decisive and buy my product. I never said that. Napoleon Hill did. I never said you need discipline to stick to your guns. I didn't tell you you need me as a mentor. Napoleon Hill told you that as a law of success. So however way you feel comfortable in delivering that chunk, do you feel that that's going to be an important chunk to put in your presentation? And does it make any difference what you're going to be selling? Why? Do they need to have those holes plugged in this pressure cooker?
